solutions.solution:
(e) According to the question,
D # R ⇒ D< R ...(i)
R * K ⇒ R≤ K ...(ii)
K @ F ⇒ K> F ...(iii)
F $ J ⇒ F≥ J ...(iv)
On combining the statements (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv), we get
D< R≤ K> F≥ J
Conclusions I. J # R ⇒ J< R (false)
II. J # K ⇒ J< K (true)
III. R # F ⇒ R< F (false)
IV. K @ D ⇒ K> D (true)
So, it is clear that Conclusions II and IV are true.

(e) According to the question,
N δ B ⇒ N= B ...(i)
B $ W ⇒ B≥ W ...(ii)
W # H ⇒ W< H ...(iii)
H * M ⇒ H≤ M ...(iv)
On combining the statements (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv), we get
N= B≥ W< H≤ M
Conclusions I. M @ W ⇒ M> W (true)
II. H @ N ⇒ H> N (false)
III. W δ N ⇒ W= N (either III or IV is true)
IV. W # N ⇒ W< N
So, it is clear that either Conclusions III or IV and I are true.

(c) According to the question,
R * D ⇒ R≤ D ...(i)
D $ J ⇒ D≥ J ...(ii)
J # M ⇒ J< M ...(iii)
M @ K ⇒ M> K ...(iv)
On combining the statements (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv), we get
R≤ D≥ J< M> K
Conclusions I. K # J ⇒ K< J (false)
II. D @ M ⇒ D> M (false)
III. R # M ⇒ R< M (false)
IV. D @ K ⇒ D> K (false)
So, it is clear that none of the conclusion is true.

(e) According to the question,
M $ K ⇒ M≥ K ...(i)
K @ N ⇒ K> N ...(ii)
N * R ⇒ N≤ R ...(iii)
R # W ⇒ R< W ...(iv)
On combining the statements (i), (ii), (iii) and (iv), we get
M≥ K> N≤ R< W
Conclusions I. W @ K ⇒ W> K (false)
II. M $ R ⇒ M≥ R (false)
III. K @ W ⇒ K> W (false)
IV. M @ N ⇒ M> N (true)
So, it is clear that only Conclusion IV is true.

(c) Given, 3M % 2N and N % 30
⇒ 3M = 2N and N = 30
⇒ 3M = 2 × 30
⇒ 20 = M ⇒ 20 % M

(c) N @ P, P # O, O @ M and N % M
⇒ N > P, P < O, O > P and N = M
⇒ M = N > P < O ⇒ O > M = N
⇒ O > N ⇒ O @ N
